design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore eight unique planets, defeat challenges, collect Compuminds to restore sentient AI network and save civilization
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Gravity manipulation and environmental hazard navigation across diverse planetary biomes with combat elements
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Intergalactic hero with laser sword traversing alien worlds; particularly empowered by inverted gravity mechanics on planet Turvia
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game explicitly rewards rule-breaking and exploitation of bugs/glitches as a design feature, except in final stage where out-of-bounds triggers freeze; encourages experimental play and treats the system as malleable rather than sacred

difficulty.lives_system: Health-based with armor upgrades providing damage mitigation across zones
difficulty.continues_system: Password system enables mid-game save progression across planets
difficulty.checkpointing: Planet-based structure allows non-linear progression with teleportation between discovered worlds
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through optional armor collection, weapon variety (laser sword, pistols, bombs), and item-based power progression; optional Communicator provides quick escape mechanic

progression.upgrade_system: Equipment-based: collectable armor (pink, green, white variants), weapons (Power Pistol, Vertigun, Maxigun, Super Sword, Crystal Bombs, Saurian Crystals, Teleport Beacon), energy containers per zone, Energy Disks
progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight planets unlock progressively; each planet contains energy container that must be obtained to advance; Compumind retrieval gates progression
replayability_hooks: Hidden shortcut spawn points reward memory and exploration
Bug exploitation and glitch utilization encouraged by design philosophy
Password system allows testing alternate equipment configurations
Multiple teleport locations per planet create route variation

world_building_techniques: Manual contains comic providing lore context for starting items
Distinct visual identities per planet (cyberpunk Cyberia, jungle Sauria, trash world Effluvia) establish world diversity
Gravity inversion on Turvia serves as environmental narrative statement about world uniqueness
Eight-planet structure implies galactic scale and colonial biotechnology infrastructure
Compumind rescue mission frames exploration as infrastructure restoration

faction_indicators: Colonial spacefaring civilization dependent on distributed AI network
danger_signals: Villain shutdown of all Compuminds represents infrastructure collapse and communication breakdown
Eight separated planets with hostile environments suggest planetary isolation and danger
Kakos (lone recovered Compumind) represents critical single point of failure
environmental_storytelling: Cyberpunk Cyberia reflects technological world with probable automation
Jungle Sauria suggests primordial life alongside civilization
Caustic trash world Effluvia implies industrial civilization with waste byproducts
Volcania, Odar named but not detailed, suggest additional environmental diversity

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - The Adventures of Rad Gravity (unused items, mechanics, level design shortcuts)
Wikipedia - The Adventures of Rad Gravity (narrative premise, world details, release info, design credits)
